chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: In this exercise, you will build a simple todo list application using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: The todo list should allow users to add new tasks by typing in an input field.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: Each task should have a delete button to remove it from the list.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: You should also add a checkbox to mark tasks as completed.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: Completed tasks should have a strikethrough text decoration.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: Use DOM manipulation to dynamically create and remove list items.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: Use event listeners to handle the add button click and delete button clicks.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: Try to also save the tasks to localStorage so they persist after page refresh.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: This exercise combines everything we have learned so far.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: Take about 45 minutes to attempt this exercise before watching the solution.

chunks.title: Exercise 3 - JavaScript Todo List
chunks.text: Building projects is the best way to learn JavaScript, so give it your best try.

text: In this exercise, you will build a simple todo list application using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The todo list should allow users to add new tasks by typing in an input field. Each task should have a delete button to remove it from the list. You should also add a checkbox to mark tasks as completed. Completed tasks should have a strikethrough text decoration. Use DOM manipulation to dynamically create and remove list items. Use event listeners to handle the add button click and delete button clicks. Try to also save the tasks to localStorage so they persist after page refresh. This exercise combines everything we have learned so far. Take about 45 minutes to attempt this exercise before watching the solution. Building projects is the best way to learn JavaScript, so give it your best try.
